metadata.name: ario_ant_escrow
metadata.description: AR.IO Trustless Escrow Program - Multi-protocol (Arweave RSA-PSS-4096 / Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1) on-chain custody for ANT NFTs, ARIO tokens, and time-locked vaults

docs: AR.IO Trustless ANT Escrow Program
Holds Metaplex Core ANT NFTs in a per-mint PDA and releases them to a
claimant designated by an Arweave RSA-PSS-4096 or Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1
signature verified fully on-chain. No off-chain authority, no oracle.
See `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md` for the full technical design and
`docs/ANT_ESCROW_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` for the phase-by-phase roadmap.

instructions.name: cancel_deposit
docs: Return the escrowed ANT to the depositor and close the escrow PDA.
Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow account being closed. `has_one = depositor` rejects calls
from anyone other than the original depositor; `close = depositor`
returns rent and zeroes the data on success.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset returning to the depositor.
`has_one`-style validation: `escrow.ant_mint` must equal this
account's key. Defends against wallet UIs that mis-route accounts.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor — sole authority for cancel. Must sign so the
system program will credit them with the closed-account rent.

instructions.name: cancel_token_deposit
docs: Return escrowed ARIO tokens to the depositor and close the
escrow PDA. Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA being closed. `has_one = depositor` rejects calls from
anyone other than the original depositor; `close = depositor`
returns rent and zeroes the data on success.

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (source of return).

accounts.name: depositor_token_account
docs: Depositor's ARIO token account (destination for returned tokens).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor -- sole authority for cancel.

instructions.name: cancel_vault_deposit
docs: Return escrowed vault tokens to the depositor. Uses ESCROW_VAULT_SEED.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA being closed. `has_one = depositor` rejects calls from
anyone other than the original depositor; `close = depositor`
returns rent and zeroes the data on success.

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (source of return).

accounts.name: depositor_token_account
docs: Depositor's ARIO token account (destination for returned tokens).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor -- sole authority for cancel.

instructions.name: claim_ant_arweave
docs: Release the ANT to `claimant` after on-chain RSA-PSS-4096
verification. Anyone can submit; only the named claimant receives
the asset. See `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md` § Cryptographic
verification → Arweave RSA-PSS for the algorithm.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA. `has_one = depositor` ties the close-rent recipient to
the original depositor account passed below; `close = depositor`
returns rent on successful claim. Cached `bump` is re-derived via
the seeds constraint for defense-in-depth.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset being released. Must match
`escrow.ant_mint` so a misrouted account can't redirect the
transfer.

accounts.name: claimant
docs: Recipient of the ANT — its pubkey is bound into the canonical
message that the Arweave key signed. Front-runners can resubmit
the tx but cannot redirect the asset.
no on-chain ownership check needed (anyone can be a claimant).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor — receives rent on escrow close. Pinned via
`has_one = depositor` on the escrow above.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Tx fee payer. Doesn't have to be the claimant — anyone can submit
a valid claim signature on the recipient's behalf.

instructions.name: claim_ant_ethereum
docs: Release the ANT to `claimant` after on-chain ECDSA secp256k1 +
EIP-191 verification. Same shape as `claim_ant_arweave`, only the
signature format and verifier differ.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA. Same constraints as `ClaimAntArweave`.

instructions.name: claim_tokens_arweave
docs: Release escrowed ARIO tokens to `claimant` after on-chain
RSA-PSS-4096 verification (Arweave path).

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA holding token deposit metadata.

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (source of tokens).

accounts.name: claimant_token_account
docs: Claimant's ARIO token account (destination).

accounts.name: claimant
docs: Recipient of the tokens -- pubkey bound into the canonical message.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor -- receives rent on escrow close.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Tx fee payer. Anyone can submit a valid claim.

instructions.name: claim_tokens_ethereum
docs: Release escrowed ARIO tokens to `claimant` after on-chain
ECDSA secp256k1 + EIP-191 verification (Ethereum path).

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA holding token deposit metadata.

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (source of tokens).

accounts.name: claimant_token_account
docs: Claimant's ARIO token account (destination).

instructions.name: claim_vault_arweave
docs: Release escrowed vault tokens to `claimant` after on-chain
RSA-PSS-4096 verification (Arweave path). If the vault is still
active, CPIs into ario-core to create a time-locked vault; if
expired, does a liquid SPL transfer.

accounts.name: claimant_token_account
docs: Claimant's ARIO token account (destination for expired-vault path).

accounts.name: payer_token_account
docs: Payer's ARIO token account (intermediate for active-vault path).

instructions.name: claim_vault_ethereum
docs: Release escrowed vault tokens to `claimant` after on-chain
ECDSA secp256k1 + EIP-191 verification (Ethereum path). Same
active/expired branching as `claim_vault_arweave`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow PDA holding vault deposit metadata.

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (source of tokens).

accounts.name: claimant_token_account
docs: Claimant's ARIO token account (destination for expired-vault path).

accounts.name: payer_token_account
docs: Payer's ARIO token account (intermediate destination for active-vault
path — payer then sends them to vaulted_transfer in a sibling ix).

accounts.name: claimant
docs: Recipient of the vault/tokens — pubkey bound into canonical message.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Original depositor — receives rent on escrow close.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Tx fee payer. For active vaults, also holds tokens temporarily
between the escrow release and the vaulted_transfer sibling ix.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: Instructions sysvar — used to verify that a matching
vaulted_transfer instruction exists in the same transaction.

instructions.name: deposit_ant
docs: Lock an ANT into escrow targeted at an Arweave or Ethereum identity.
`recipient_protocol`: `0` = Arweave RSA-PSS-4096, `1` = Ethereum ECDSA.
`recipient_pubkey`: 512-byte RSA modulus (Arweave) or 20-byte address
(Ethereum); any other (protocol, length) pair is rejected.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: New escrow PDA, one per ANT mint. `init` enforces "no double
deposit" — a second `deposit_ant` for the same mint fails because
the PDA already exists.

accounts.name: ant_asset
docs: The Metaplex Core asset (ANT) being escrowed. Constrained to the
mpl-core program so callers can't pass a system account or a
different program's NFT. Inner-data discriminator is checked in
the handler via `read_mpl_core_owner`.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Caller — must be the current ANT owner. Pays rent + tx fee + signs
the mpl-core TransferV1 CPI.

accounts.name: mpl_core_program
docs: Metaplex Core program. Pinned to MPL_CORE_PROGRAM_ID so the CPI
can't be redirected.

instructions.name: deposit_tokens
docs: Deposit ARIO tokens into escrow for a designated Arweave or
Ethereum recipient. Tokens transfer from the depositor's ATA to
the escrow PDA's ATA.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: New escrow PDA for this token deposit.

accounts.name: depositor_token_account
docs: Depositor's ARIO token account (source of tokens).

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (destination). Must be pre-created
by the client (via createAssociatedTokenAccountIdempotent in the
same tx). Owner must be the escrow PDA.

accounts.name: ario_mint
docs: The ARIO SPL token mint.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor — pays rent + tx fee + signs SPL transfer.

instructions.name: deposit_vault
docs: Deposit ARIO tokens into escrow as a time-locked vault for a
designated Arweave or Ethereum recipient.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: New escrow PDA for this vault deposit.

accounts.name: depositor_token_account
docs: Depositor's ARIO token account (source of tokens).

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow PDA's ARIO token account (destination). Must be pre-created.

accounts.name: ario_mint
docs: The ARIO SPL token mint.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor -- pays rent + tx fee + signs SPL transfer.

instructions.name: update_recipient
docs: Re-target the escrow at a different recipient identity. Rotates the
nonce so any in-flight claim sigs bound to the prior recipient are
invalidated. Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow being re-targeted. `has_one = depositor` enforces the
authority check and the `seeds`/`bump = escrow.bump` constraint
re-derives the PDA against the cached bump (cheaper than
`find_program_address`).

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor — sole authority for `update_recipient`.

instructions.name: update_token_recipient
docs: Re-target a token/vault escrow at a different recipient identity.
Rotates the nonce. Only callable by `escrow.depositor`.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow being re-targeted. `has_one = depositor` enforces the
authority check; seeds/bump re-derive the PDA.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor -- sole authority for `update_token_recipient`.

instructions.name: update_vault_recipient
docs: Re-target a vault escrow at a different recipient identity.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow being re-targeted. `has_one = depositor` enforces the
authority check; seeds/bump re-derive the PDA.

accounts.name: depositor
docs: Depositor -- sole authority for `update_vault_recipient`.

errors.name: InvalidRecipientProtocol
errors.msg: Invalid recipient protocol (must be 0=Arweave or 1=Ethereum)

errors.name: InvalidRecipientPubkeyLength
errors.msg: Invalid recipient pubkey length for the given protocol

errors.name: NotDepositor
errors.msg: Unauthorized: only the original depositor can perform this action

errors.name: InvalidAsset
errors.msg: Invalid Metaplex Core asset (wrong owner program or asset discriminator)

errors.name: NotAntOwner
errors.msg: Unauthorized: caller is not the current ANT owner

errors.name: AntMintMismatch
errors.msg: Escrow PDA does not correspond to the supplied ANT mint

errors.name: NonceMismatch
errors.msg: Claim signature nonce does not match the escrow's current nonce

errors.name: ProtocolMismatch
errors.msg: Wrong claim path: this escrow targets a different protocol

errors.name: SignatureVerificationFailed
errors.msg: Signature verification failed

errors.name: InvalidSaltLength
errors.msg: Invalid PSS salt length (must be ≤ 32 bytes)

errors.name: EthereumAddressMismatch
errors.msg: Recovered Ethereum address does not match recipient

errors.name: EcdsaHighS
errors.msg: ECDSA signature is malleable (high-S form rejected per EIP-2)

errors.name: InvalidRecoveryId
errors.msg: Invalid ECDSA recovery id

errors.name: AmountZero
errors.msg: Amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: VaultDurationTooShort
errors.msg: Vault lock duration below minimum

errors.name: VaultAmountBelowMinimum
errors.msg: Vault amount below minimum (100 ARIO)

errors.name: AssetTypeMismatch
errors.msg: Asset type mismatch

errors.name: MintMismatch
errors.msg: ARIO mint mismatch

errors.name: TokenAccountOwnerMismatch
errors.msg: Token account owner does not match the expected account

errors.name: ArithmeticOverflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: MissingVaultedTransferInstruction
errors.msg: Missing or invalid vaulted_transfer instruction in transaction

types.name: EscrowAnt
docs: Per-escrow custody record. One PDA per escrowed ANT, derived from
`[ESCROW_ANT_SEED, ant_mint]`. Layout matches `docs/ANT_ESCROW_DESIGN.md`
§ Account model byte-for-byte (653 bytes payload + 8-byte Anchor
discriminator = 661 bytes on-chain).
The `_reserved` tail and the explicit `version` byte are forward-compat
hooks so additive field changes can ship without a `realloc` migration.

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version. v1 = `ESCROW_VERSION_V1`.

fields.name: bump
docs: Cached PDA bump. Faster than `find_program_address` on signer-seed
reconstruction during transfer CPIs.

fields.name: depositor
docs: Wallet that called `deposit_ant`. Sole authority for
`cancel_deposit` and `update_recipient`; receives rent on close.

fields.name: ant_mint
docs: Metaplex Core asset (ANT) mint pubkey held in custody.
Denormalized from PDA seeds for defense-in-depth account validation.

fields.name: recipient_protocol
docs: `0` = Arweave RSA-PSS-4096, `1` = Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey_len
docs: Active length (in bytes) of the recipient pubkey blob below.
Constrained at write time to {512, 20} per protocol.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey
docs: Recipient identity bytes, zero-padded to `RECIPIENT_PUBKEY_MAX_LEN`.
- Arweave: 512-byte big-endian RSA modulus (the JWK `n` field).
- Ethereum: 20-byte Keccak address followed by 492 zero bytes.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Anti-replay nonce. Initial value:
`sha256(deposit_slot_le_bytes || ant_mint || depositor)`
Rotated by `update_recipient` to invalidate any in-flight claim sigs.

fields.name: deposit_slot
docs: Slot at deposit. Informational only — used as a salt for nonce
derivation but not consulted on claim. Useful for indexers and
support tooling.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Reserved bytes. Hold zero until repurposed in a future schema bump.

types.name: EscrowToken
docs: Escrow account for ARIO tokens (liquid) or vaulted positions (time-locked).
The `asset_type` field discriminates between a liquid token transfer
(claim sends tokens to claimant's ATA) and a vaulted transfer (claim
CPIs into `ario-core::vaulted_transfer` to create a time-locked vault
for the claimant with the remaining lock duration).
PDA seeds:
Token: ["escrow_token", depositor, asset_id]
Vault: ["escrow_vault", depositor, asset_id]
`asset_id` is a client-supplied 32-byte identifier. For the migration
batch, it's deterministic: `sha256("token-escrow:" + arweave_addr)` or
`sha256("vault-escrow:" + arweave_addr + ":" + vault_id)`.

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version. v1 = 1.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: depositor
docs: Wallet that deposited the tokens.

fields.name: asset_type
docs: `ASSET_TYPE_TOKEN` (1) or `ASSET_TYPE_VAULT` (2).

fields.name: amount
docs: Amount of ARIO (mARIO) held in escrow.

fields.name: ario_mint
docs: The ARIO SPL token mint. Validated at deposit time.

fields.name: asset_id
docs: Client-supplied unique identifier for this escrow. Part of PDA seeds.

fields.name: recipient_protocol
docs: `0` = Arweave RSA-PSS-4096, `1` = Ethereum ECDSA secp256k1.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey_len
docs: Active length of the recipient pubkey blob.

fields.name: recipient_pubkey
docs: Recipient identity bytes, zero-padded to 512.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Anti-replay nonce.

fields.name: deposit_slot
docs: Slot at deposit.

fields.name: vault_end_timestamp
docs: For vault escrows: the unix timestamp at which the vault unlocks.
Zero for liquid token escrows.

fields.name: vault_revocable
docs: For vault escrows: whether the vault is revocable.
False for liquid token escrows.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Reserved for future fields.
